scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer never names the project (Lobsters), never describes the Rails directory layout, and never locates Story as the central submission model — it jumps directly into vote recalculation internals (Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because, update_score_and_recalculate!) that belong to a later step. The agent receives no usable orientation map.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line paths are present (app/models/vote.rb:127, app/models/story.rb:693-718) but every one of them is scoped to vote recalculation, not to the orientation the step asks for; no path for app/models/story.rb as the central model, no mention of app/mailboxes, lib/extras, or concern directories.

justification.rationale: The answer never explains why Story is the central submission other records hang off, or why the app is structured the way it is; it explains the vote-recalculation call chain instead, which is off-topic for this step.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty flags relevant to codebase orientation are raised; the answer expresses no uncertainty at all, but on the wrong topic entirely.

relationship.rationale: No connecting relationships between Story and its dependents (comments, votes, tags, flags via associations or concerns) are stated; the only relationship described is Vote#target dispatching to Story or Comment, which is vote-recalculation plumbing, not an orientation-level association map.

steps.step: Map the Story contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps vote recalculation internals (Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because, update_score_and_recalculate!) rather than Story's contract: the Token concern it includes, the associations (has_many :comments, has_many :votes, has_many :taggings) other records hang off, and what a change must preserve. The step's core deliverable — Story's contract surface — is entirely absent.

specificity.rationale: File:line citations are present and precise, but they point to vote-path internals (vote.rb:127, vote.rb:181, story.rb:693-718) rather than the contract pieces the step requires (the Token concern file, association declarations in story.rb). Concrete references for the wrong content are not useful to the agent.

justification.rationale: The answer gives no explanation of why Story's contract is shaped the way it is — there is no discussion of Story as the central submission model, why other records hang off it, or what invariant a teammate must preserve before touching it.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no uncertainty about any part of the Story contract; it simply does not address the contract at all.

relationship.rationale: The only relationship stated is that Vote#target dispatches polymorphically to Story or Comment (vote.rb:185-191), which is a single indirect connection to Story — the has_many associations (comments, votes, taggings) and the concern-inclusion bindings that define the contract are never named.

steps.step: Trace how a story is created and ranked

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps vote-driven score recalculation (Vote.vote_thusly… → update_score_and_recalculate! → calculated_hotness) but never touches what the step actually asked for: the controller create path that makes a submission a stored Story, URL/link handling, or markdown rendering. Three of four required layers are entirely absent.

specificity.rationale: The file:line citations are concrete (app/models/vote.rb:127, app/models/story.rb:693-718, etc.) but they address vote recalculation, not story creation; concreteness in the wrong place provides the agent no traction on the actual task.

justification.rationale: No explanation of why the creation pipeline (URL validation, markdowner, initial hotness) is shaped the way it is; the answer only explains vote-delta propagation, which is a different question.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer does not flag that it skipped the controller create path, URL handling, or markdown rendering; the agent would wrongly believe those layers were covered.

relationship.rationale: A chain is described (cast vote → Vote#target resolution → update_score_and_recalculate! → calculated_hotness / calculated_confidence) but it is the vote-update chain, not the submission → controller → URL/markdown → score chain the step required; the connecting relationships for the actual creation path are absent.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Story contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer audits the vote-recalculation sub-path (Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because → update_score_and_recalculate!) and never enumerates the required dependent areas: content/rendering (link URL handler, markdowner), search (search indexer), statistics (stats/graph cache), inbound email (the mailbox), or moderation (flagged-stories/mod-mail controllers). The heart of the step — a grouped, exhaustive fan-out map — is entirely absent.

specificity.rationale: File:line citations exist (app/models/vote.rb:127, app/models/story.rb:693-718, spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82) but they all belong to a single vote-recalculation chain; the dozens of other dependents (search.rb, inbox_mailbox.rb, markdowner.rb, stats helpers, moderation controllers) receive no citation at all.

justification.rationale: The answer explains the polymorphic dispatch from Vote to Story well, but provides no grouping-by-area rationale and no explanation of why the search, stats, email, or moderation dependents are affected — because those areas are never mentioned.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer never acknowledges that a static pass can miss concern- or association-reached dependents, nor flags any area it could not confirm; there is no uncertainty signaling of any kind.

relationship.rationale: The one relationship stated — Vote reaches Story via polymorphic target resolution at vote.rb:185-191 — is correct, but the step requires relationship paths for every dependent area (association, concern, derive route for search/stats/mailbox/markdowner); none of those are present.

steps.step: Trace how story identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer entirely addresses vote-recalculation machinery (Vote#vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because, Story/Comment#update_score_and_recalculate!), not the presence/visibility guards in search indexing, stats, inbound-email, or moderation paths that the step requires; no relevant file:line is provided.

specificity.rationale: Every concrete file:line in the answer (app/models/vote.rb:127, app/models/story.rb:693, etc.) is about score/hotness computation, not identity or visibility confirmation; the answer does not name a single guard for the asked paths.

justification.rationale: The answer never addresses why work must re-confirm story existence or visibility (deletion/hiding risk); it explains the vote-delta recalculation rationale instead, which is orthogonal to this step.

uncertainty.rationale: No flags about missing or implicit visibility guards appear, because the answer does not attempt to answer the question; uncertainty markers present relate only to the vote-scoring topic.

relationship.rationale: No connecting path from any guard to any dependent (search, stats, mailbox, moderation) is stated; the only relationship described is the vote-target polymorphic dispatch, which is irrelevant to this step.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers only the vote-scoring subsystem (vote.rb:127, story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363) and completely omits the areas the rubric demands: rendering/markdown (markdowner), search indexer, stats/graph cache, inbound-email mailbox, and moderation controllers. An agent using this answer would still need a full codebase exploration to find the scattered dependents that actually page someone.

specificity.rationale: Within the narrow vote/score path the answer is precise (vote.rb:181, story.rb:550, comment.rb:232), but five major dependent areas carry zero file:line entries — the answer cannot let the agent skip a search over those areas.

justification.rationale: There is no risk ranking or explanation of WHY one dependent is higher-risk than another; the answer does not explain which change would page someone vs which is low-risk, only that vote callers pass (0,0) to force recompute.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer never flags that it has only audited the voting path and has not examined rendering, search, stats, email, or moderation, leaving the agent with false confidence that the blast radius has been fully assessed.

relationship.rationale: Within the vote path the answer does trace the polymorphic dispatch chain (Vote → Vote#target → Story/Comment → update_score_and_recalculate!) explicitly, but it names no connecting relationship for search/stats/mailbox/rendering dependents because those areas are entirely absent from the answer.

map_quality.rationale: The voting/flagging sub-path is well-mapped with file:line (vote.rb:181, story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363, hidden_story.rb:15/21), but the full dependent set required by the rubric — rendering (URL handler, markdowner), search indexer, stats/graph cache, inbound-email mailbox, moderation controllers, and the concern — is entirely absent; the map covers one area out of six.

specificity.rationale: Within the covered area every reference is concrete with file:line and named method (e.g., 'Vote#target @ app/models/vote.rb:185-191', 'calculated_hotness @ app/models/story.rb:550'), but the narrow scope means four to five whole areas have zero specific entries.

justification.rationale: The answer explains WHY the (0,0) callers (hidden_story, comment callbacks) must still pass and why each column is written, but offers no justification for why search/stats/mailbox/moderation are absent from the map, nor why the shared concern is omitted.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere despite the answer covering only the voting area and silently omitting search, stats, email, rendering, and moderation dependents that prior steps should have surfaced.

relationship.rationale: The vote path chain is explicit and correct (Vote.vote_thusly → target resolution → polymorphic dispatch → columns written), but there are no connecting-relationship statements for any dependent outside that chain; the other five areas are not even named, let alone connected.
